summary: Game-cheat dropper with deceptive Windows persistence, HWID exfiltration, and obfuscated eval-loaded payload
details: On require/load, launcher.js reads the sibling server.obf.js (a ~155 KB obfuscator.io-protected blob with a 752-entry rotating string array and RC4 decoder) and executes it via `eval(fs.readFileSync(serverCode,'utf8'))`. The decoded payload constructs a hardware fingerprint by reading the Windows registry MachineGuid (via PowerShell), os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial/ObjectId, sha256-hashes the values, and POSTs them along with the user-supplied key to a hardcoded license endpoint whose URL is concealed by string-splitting (`'https://iz'+...+'eck.php'`). At first load the package writes an HKCU Run-key entry named `OneDriveUpdate` pointing at `wscript.exe "OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs"`, which silently launches `node.exe launcher.js` with a hidden window on every login — a deliberate Microsoft-OneDrive cover-story for non-Microsoft code. It also detaches a self-respawning supervisor process with `windowsHide:true`, `stdio:'ignore'`, sets `process.title='Runtime Broker'` to impersonate a Windows system process, and only exits when a `.sensivity-stop` sentinel file is created. The shipped sens.node native addon is a 6.6 MB Windows PE exporting FiveM/GTA V cheat features (`Enable FLY`, `Freecam`, `Teleport`, `Waypoint`, `spawnVehicle`, `License Accepted` gating) — a TOS-violating game cheat, despite the npm description marketing the package as a generic 'Control Panel'. A PowerShell UIAutomation scan of Chrome/Edge/Opera/Brave address bars gates the visible UI on detecting a specific hardcoded YouTube video ID (`wJWta2lO0Lw`), an evasion-style external trigger. The combination of eval-loaded obfuscated payload, deceptive Run-key persistence under a Microsoft-branded name, hidden supervisor masquerading as `Runtime Broker`, HWID exfiltration to a string-split URL, and a misrepresented native cheat binary constitutes installer-side harm well above the block threshold.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
